title: Lithocarpus celebicus
intro: Lithocarpus celebicus, also known as Celebes Lithocarpus or Celebes Bur oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus celebicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus celebicus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus celebicus is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus and is a type of evergreen tree native to Sulawesi, Indonesia. The classification of Lithocarpus celebicus places it within the order Fagales and the kingdom Plantae. This classification highlights its connection to other deciduous and evergreen trees in the Fagaceae family. At the most basic taxonomic level, Lithocarpus celebicus is also classified as a member of the domain Eukarya.
